# mlir-opt --convert-memref-to-spirv crashes on tensor.from_elements

**Author:** mlirBug
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/178217
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** mlir, crash, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2026-03-11T10:40:50Z

## Body

mlir-opt version: 22.1.0-rc1

The crash is triggered while processing a `tensor.from_elements` operation producing a tensor<4xindex>. The pass internally casts an Attribute to IntegerAttr incorrectly, causing a hard assertion failure.

```mlir
module {
  func.func @main() {
    %0 = ub.poison : index
    %1 = spirv.Undef : !spirv.array<4 x !spirv.array<4 x i32>>
    %2 = tensor.from_elements %0, %0, %0, %0 : tensor<4xindex>
    return
  }
}
```
```
mlir-opt --convert-memref-to-spirv
```
```
mlir-opt: llvm-project-llvmorg-22.1.0-rc1/llvm/include/llvm/Support/Casting.h:560: decltype(auto) llvm::cast(const From&) [with To = mlir::IntegerAttr; From = mlir::Attribute]: Assertion `isa<To>(Val) && "cast<Ty>() argument of incompatible type!"' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace and instructions to reproduce the bug.
Stack dump:
0.      Program arguments: llvmorg-22.1.0-install/bin/mlir-opt --convert-memref-to-spirv test.mlir
 #0 0x000064ffe0536e32 llvm::sys::PrintStackTrace(llvm::raw_ostream&, int) (llvmorg-22.1.0-install/bin/mlir-opt+0x252fe32)
 #1 0x000064ffe05335af llvm::sys::RunSignalHandlers() (llvmorg-22.1.0-install/bin/mlir-opt+0x252c5af)
 #2 0x000064ffe05336fc SignalHandler(int, siginfo_t*, void*) Signals.cpp:0:0
 #3 0x0000704c4d445330 (/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x45330)
 #4 0x0000704c4d49eb2c __pthread_kill_implementation ./nptl/pthread_kill.c:44:76
 #5 0x0000704c4d49eb2c __pthread_kill_internal ./nptl/pthread_kill.c:78:10
 #6 0x0000704c4d49eb2c pthread_kill ./nptl/pthread_kill.c:89:10
 #7 0x0000704c4d44527e raise ./signal/../sysdeps/posix/raise.c:27:6
 #8 0x0000704c4d4288ff abort ./stdlib/abort.c:81:7
 #9 0x0000704c4d42881b _nl_load_domain ./intl/loadmsgcat.c:1177:9
#10 0x0000704c4d43b517 (/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x3b517)
```
